<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tachibanazuka Kofun]][[en:Tachibanazuka Kofun]][[ja:橘塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109364122]] Wikidata name: Tachibanazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tachibanazuka Kofun | native_name =橘塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tachibanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tachibanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tachibanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.705000000000005|N|130.9163888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|42|18|N|130|54|59|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic [[:en:Granite|Granite]] megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.miyako.lg.jp/rekisiminnzoku/kankou/kohun/kohun_3.html Miyako Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://miyako-museum.jp/list/detail.php?uniq_id=17 Miyako Digital Museum] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13194 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}
Tachibanazuka Kofun

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic [[:en:Granite|Granite]] megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tachibanazuka Kofun]][[en:Tachibanazuka Kofun]][[ja:橘塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109364122]] Wikidata name: Tachibanazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tachibanazuka Kofun | native_name =橘塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tachibanazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tachibanazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tachibanazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.705000000000005|N|130.9163888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|42|18|N|130|54|59|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic [[:en:Granite|Granite]] megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic [[:en:Granite|Granite]] megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic [[:en:Granite|Granite]] megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.miyako.lg.jp/rekisiminnzoku/kankou/kohun/kohun_3.html Miyako Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://miyako-museum.jp/list/detail.php?uniq_id=17 Miyako Digital Museum] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13194 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near [[:en:Japan National Route 201|Japan National Route 201]] and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of [[:en:Imagawa-Kappa Station|Imagawa-Kappa Station]] on the [[:en:Heisei Chikuhō Railway|Heisei Chikuhō Railway]] [[:en:Tagawa Line|Tagawa Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Miyako, Fukuoka|Miyako]], [[:en:Miyako District, Fukuoka|Miyako District]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>

From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near {{ill|Japan National Route 201|en|Japan National Route 201|WD=Q11421419|lt=Japan National Route 201}} and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of {{ill|Imagawa-Kappa Station|en|Imagawa-Kappa Station|WD=Q11376925|lt=Imagawa-Kappa Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|WD=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}} {{ill|Tagawa Line|en|Tagawa Line|WD=Q11482938|lt=Tagawa Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Miyako, Fukuoka|en|Miyako, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206868|lt=Miyako}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near {{ill|Japan National Route 201|en|Japan National Route 201|WD=Q11421419|lt=Japan National Route 201}} and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of {{ill|Imagawa-Kappa Station|en|Imagawa-Kappa Station|WD=Q11376925|lt=Imagawa-Kappa Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|WD=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}} {{ill|Tagawa Line|en|Tagawa Line|WD=Q11482938|lt=Tagawa Line}}.

It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near {{ill|Japan National Route 201|en|Japan National Route 201|WD=Q11421419|lt=Japan National Route 201}} and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of {{ill|Imagawa-Kappa Station|en|Imagawa-Kappa Station|WD=Q11376925|lt=Imagawa-Kappa Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|WD=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}} {{ill|Tagawa Line|en|Tagawa Line|WD=Q11482938|lt=Tagawa Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Miyako, Fukuoka|en|Miyako, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206868|lt=Miyako}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near {{ill|Japan National Route 201|en|Japan National Route 201|WD=Q11421419|lt=Japan National Route 201|1=Japan National Route 201}} and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of {{ill|Imagawa-Kappa Station|en|Imagawa-Kappa Station|WD=Q11376925|lt=Imagawa-Kappa Station|1=Imagawa-Kappa Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|WD=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway|1=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}} {{ill|Tagawa Line|en|Tagawa Line|WD=Q11482938|lt=Tagawa Line|1=Tagawa Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tachibanazuka Kofun'''|橘塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Miyako, Fukuoka|en|Miyako, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206868|lt=Miyako|1=Miyako, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District|1=Miyako District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tachibanazuka Kofun is located in a corner of the schoolyard of Miyako Town Kuroda Elementary School, about 500 meters east of [[Ayazuka Kofun]]. It is a tomb made of gigantic {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} megaliths. Since it has been open for a long time, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered. It was previously thought to be an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, but research from 1995 revealed that it was actually a {{nihongo|''hofun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus, measuring 37 meters from north-to-south and 52 meters from east-to-west with a height of 3.8 meters. The structure of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is very similar to that of the nearby Ayazuka Kofun. From the structure of the burial chamber, it is believed to be slightly older than the Ayazuka Kofun, and was built around the end of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in a corner of Kuroda Elementary School's schoolyard, near {{ill|Japan National Route 201|en|Japan National Route 201|WD=Q11421419|lt=Japan National Route 201|1=Japan National Route 201}} and approximately 4.6 kilometers due west of {{ill|Imagawa-Kappa Station|en|Imagawa-Kappa Station|WD=Q11376925|lt=Imagawa-Kappa Station|1=Imagawa-Kappa Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|WD=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway|1=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}} {{ill|Tagawa Line|en|Tagawa Line|WD=Q11482938|lt=Tagawa Line|1=Tagawa Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
